questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Derision
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Derision means contemptuous ridicule or mockery. The most appropriate antonym would be admiration, which conveys respect or approval, the opposite of ridicule. The meanings of other options are: Disintegration: Refers to breaking apart or falling into pieces, unrelated to the concept of ridicule or mockery. Termination: Refers to ending something, which is not related to derision. Cynical: Describes a distrustful or skeptical attitude, which does not serve as the opposite of derision.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Most probably, / she come / to the restaurant / next month.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The verb "come" is incorrectly used here. The correct form should be "she will come" to indicate a future action. The corrected sentence is: "Most probably, she will come to the restaurant next month."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the sentence that has the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Kill two birds with one stone
options: He was so unlucky that he never won a single coin toss in the entire tournament.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Today’s fishing session was a success as both the fishermen were working together.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Maya decided to clean the house but at the end of the day, she realised it was a great workout.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} He ruined his chances of getting into college as he got distracted while studying for two different entrance tests and not perfecting even a single one.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer will be: Maya decided to clean the house but at the end of the day, she realised it was a great workout. “Kill two birds with one stone” is an idiom that means to achieve two things in one action.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The people were gradually realizing that social gatherings were (draining).
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Draining means something that is exhausting or depleting while absorbing means something that captures interest or attention in a way that is engaging and not tiring. These two words are opposites in the context of how they affect the people involved. The meanings of other words are: Flippant: Refers to a lack of seriousness, which does not relate to the opposite of draining. Unfruitful: Refers to something that is not productive or effective, but it is not the opposite of draining. Frivolous: Refers to something that is not serious or important, but it does not directly oppose the idea of draining.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Aparna will have participate / in the singing competition / by the time her father/returns from the tour.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} by the time her father
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The phrase "will have participate" is incorrect because after "will have," the verb should be in the past participle form. The correct form is "will have participated." The corrected sentence is: "Aparna will have participated in the singing competition by the time her father returns from the tour."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined words. Since God is (an all-powerful) creator, he has absolute control over those whom he has created.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Omnipotent means all-powerful or having unlimited power, which fits perfectly in the context of the sentence, where God is described as having absolute control over creation. The other options are: Voracious: Refers to having a very eager approach, usually to food, and does not relate to power. Omniscient: Means all-knowing, which is different from being all-powerful. Incorrigible: Means incapable of being corrected or reformed, which does not relate to the meaning of being all-powerful.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. Nitika asked the doctor if she could get the discharge in the morning.
options: Nitika asked the doctor, "Can I get the discharge in the morning?"
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Nitika asked the doctor, "How can I get the discharge in the morning?"
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Nitika asked the doctor, "Could I have got the discharge in the morning?"
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Nitika asked the doctor, "If I can get the discharge in the morning?"
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Indirect speech: "Nitika asked the doctor if she could get the discharge in the morning." Direct speech: "Can I get the discharge in the morning?" The question in indirect speech begins with "if", indicating that it was a yes/no question. The "if" is dropped in direct speech, and the verb "could" is replaced by "can" to form a direct question.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the CORRECTLY spelled word.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The correctly spelled word is: Sovereignty. Sovereignty: The authority of a state to govern itself or another state. It refers to supreme power or authority, particularly in the context of countries or governing bodies. The other options are: Itinerary (correct spelling for "Itinarary"): A detailed plan or route of a journey, often listing the places to be visited and the times of travel. Grievance (correct spelling for "Greivance"): A complaint or a feeling of resentment over something believed to be wrong or unfair, typically one that leads to a formal complaint. Renaissance: (The correct spelling for "Rennaaissance") - A period of cultural revival and renewed interest in classical art, literature, and learning that began in Italy during the 14th century and spread across Europe. It can also refer to a revival of art, literature, or culture in any period.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active/passive voice. The child will recite the poem.
options: By child poem will be recited.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The poem will be recited by the child.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The poem is recited by the child.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The poem will recite by the child.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Active voice: "The child will recite the poem." Passive voice: The object of the active sentence ("the poem") becomes the subject in the passive sentence. The correct passive construction is: "The poem will be recited by the child."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Read the following sentence and select its future tense form from the following options. The toy company promoted newly launched products at affordable prices.
options: The toy company is promoting newly launched products at affordable prices.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The toy company promotes newly launched products at affordable prices.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The toy company will be promoting newly launched products at affordable prices.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The toy company had promoted newly launched products at affordable prices.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The original sentence ("The toy company promoted newly launched products at affordable prices.") is in the past tense. To convert it into the future tense, we need to use "will be promoting," which indicates an ongoing action in the future. The correct future tense form is: The toy company will be promoting newly launched products at affordable prices.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active/passive voice. My shoes have been stolen.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Somebody has stolen my shoes.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The original sentence ("My shoes have been stolen.") is in the passive voice. In passive voice, the object ("my shoes") becomes the subject, and the focus is on the action rather than the doer. To express the sentence in active voice, we need to identify the doer of the action. In this case, the doer is "somebody" and the verb should match the tense of the original sentence (present perfect tense). The correct option is: Somebody has stolen my shoes.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. Jayesh said, “Let us wait for the cab.”
options: Jayesh proposed that they should wait for the cab.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Jayesh proposed that they should has wait for the cab.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Jayesh proposes that let we waited for the cab.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Jayesh propose that they should been wait for the cab.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The original sentence ("Jayesh said, 'Let us wait for the cab.'") is in direct speech, where Jayesh is directly quoted. To convert it into indirect speech, we report what Jayesh said, using a verb like "proposed" and modifying the pronouns accordingly ("they should" instead of "us"). The correct option is: Jayesh proposed that they should wait for the cab.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. Reptiles ______ from amphibians. For a long period of 160 million years in Earth's history, reptiles were the dominant form of life on the planet and _______ in an enormous variety of shapes and sizes.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: "Evolved" is the most appropriate verb in the first blank because it implies that reptiles developed or changed from amphibians over time, which fits the context. "Existed" is the correct verb for the second blank because it refers to reptiles being present or living in a variety of shapes and sizes.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Despite numerous setbacks and rejections, he refused to give up on his dream of making it big. B. John had always been a talented musician, but he struggled to find his place in the competitive and cutthroat music industry. C. With the help of a supportive community of fellow musicians and fans, John continued to hone his craft and develop his own unique sound. D. Eventually, his hard work paid off and he landed a record deal with a major label.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: B introduces John as a talented musician struggling to find his place in the industry. A elaborates on his persistence and refusal to give up despite setbacks. C follows by showing how the support from the music community helped him develop his craft. D concludes with the success he achieved by landing a record deal. The correct sequence is BACD.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined words. I suppose dancing that well would require a lot of (actual application).
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentence:- I suppose dancing that well would require a lot of actual application. Explanation: The correct one-word substitute for "actual application" in the sentence is practice. So the sentence would read: "I suppose dancing that well would require a lot of practice." Practice refers to the repeated exercise or performance of an activity to improve skills, which fits the context of dancing.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To cross swords
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The correct meaning of the idiom "to cross swords" is "to fight." This idiom is used to describe a situation where two people argue, conflict, or have a verbal fight.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the word in the given sentence. The book narrated the dancer's (prowess) in a lucid manner.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The most appropriate synonym of the word "prowess" in the given sentence is "Exceptional mastery." The word "prowess" refers to skill or expertise, especially in a particular activity or field. "Exceptional mastery" conveys the same meaning of having great skill or ability.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Ashwagandha is an adaptogenic herb that has multiple benefits like improving thyroid functioning and reducing blood sugar levels. B. But remember to consult a specialist while deciding the dosage and duration of continued intake. C. It relaxes the nervous system and lowers cortisol (stress hormone) levels in the blood, thus making you feel calm and relaxed overall. D. You can consume Ashwagandha powder or Ashwagandha capsules.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: A introduces Ashwagandha and its benefits. C elaborates on how Ashwagandha works by relaxing the nervous system and lowering cortisol levels. D provides the methods of consuming Ashwagandha. B advises consulting a specialist about dosage. The right answer is indeed ACDB.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The last lady standing was _____, a fully paid vacation to Australia.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Rewarded: To give someone a reward for something they have done, typically as a form of recognition or compensation. The sentence would read: "The last lady standing was rewarded, a fully paid vacation to Australia." This makes sense because "rewarded" indicates that the lady received a reward (the vacation) for her effort. The meaning of other options are: Appreciated: To recognize the value or worth of something or someone. It can also mean to express gratitude or approval. Discarded: To throw away or get rid of something that is no longer useful or wanted. Allotted: To assign or distribute something (like time, resources, or tasks) in a particular way or amount.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Plunge
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: Plunge: To dive or fall suddenly and quickly: It refers to a sudden downward movement, like jumping or falling into something (e.g., "He plunged into the pool"). Dive: Means to jump or fall quickly into something, which is similar to the meaning of "plunge." The meanings of other options are: Transgress: Means to violate a rule, law, or moral code. It's not related to "plunge." Rush: Means to move quickly, but it doesn't necessarily imply diving or plunging into something. Fly: Means to move through the air, which is unrelated to the concept of plunging.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Students put their heart and soul into studies while (1)_________ for examinations. They prepare the syllabus by giving many revisions, making important points on flashcards, and preparing notes throughout the year. They read reference books in order to score good marks in the examination. This is the demanding (2)__________ of all the students who are appearing in the examinations. But, most students feel tense on the day before an examination. Everyone is in a state of (3)_________ as to what questions would be asked in the paper and whether they could write their answers properly to get good marks. Such overthinking is the cause of (4)________ which definitely affects their performance. They should revise the subject regularly and should not keep their doubts pending. This will not create a (5)________ of backlog and they will get sufficient time to resolve their problem. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The sentence discusses students getting ready for their examinations, which is best described as "preparing" for the exams. Correct sentence: "Students put their heart and soul into studies while preparing for examinations."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Students put their heart and soul into studies while (1)_________ for examinations. They prepare the syllabus by giving many revisions, making important points on flashcards, and preparing notes throughout the year. They read reference books in order to score good marks in the examination. This is the demanding (2)__________ of all the students who are appearing in the examinations. But, most students feel tense on the day before an examination. Everyone is in a state of (3)_________ as to what questions would be asked in the paper and whether they could write their answers properly to get good marks. Such overthinking is the cause of (4)________ which definitely affects their performance. They should revise the subject regularly and should not keep their doubts pending. This will not create a (5)________ of backlog and they will get sufficient time to resolve their problem. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The passage describes the daily actions and preparations of students for examinations, which fits with the idea of a "routine" — the regular and demanding tasks students engage in while preparing for exams. Correct sentence: "This is the demanding routine of all the students who are appearing in the examinations."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Students put their heart and soul into studies while (1)_________ for examinations. They prepare the syllabus by giving many revisions, making important points on flashcards, and preparing notes throughout the year. They read reference books in order to score good marks in the examination. This is the demanding (2)__________ of all the students who are appearing in the examinations. But, most students feel tense on the day before an examination. Everyone is in a state of (3)_________ as to what questions would be asked in the paper and whether they could write their answers properly to get good marks. Such overthinking is the cause of (4)________ which definitely affects their performance. They should revise the subject regularly and should not keep their doubts pending. This will not create a (5)________ of backlog and they will get sufficient time to resolve their problem. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The passage mentions that students feel tense about what questions might be asked and whether they can answer them correctly. This reflects a sense of "uncertainty" about the exam, as they are unsure about the outcome. Correct sentence: "Everyone is in a state of uncertainty as to what questions would be asked in the paper and whether they could write their answers properly to get good marks."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Students put their heart and soul into studies while (1)_________ for examinations. They prepare the syllabus by giving many revisions, making important points on flashcards, and preparing notes throughout the year. They read reference books in order to score good marks in the examination. This is the demanding (2)__________ of all the students who are appearing in the examinations. But, most students feel tense on the day before an examination. Everyone is in a state of (3)_________ as to what questions would be asked in the paper and whether they could write their answers properly to get good marks. Such overthinking is the cause of (4)________ which definitely affects their performance. They should revise the subject regularly and should not keep their doubts pending. This will not create a (5)________ of backlog and they will get sufficient time to resolve their problem. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: The passage discusses students' overthinking, which leads to negative effects on their performance. "Anxiety" refers to the feeling of worry or nervousness, which fits the context of students feeling tense and overthinking about the exam. Correct sentence: "Such overthinking is the cause of anxiety which definitely affects their performance."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate words to complete the passage. For each blank, select the most appropriate word from the given option. Students put their heart and soul into studies while (1)_________ for examinations. They prepare the syllabus by giving many revisions, making important points on flashcards, and preparing notes throughout the year. They read reference books in order to score good marks in the examination. This is the demanding (2)__________ of all the students who are appearing in the examinations. But, most students feel tense on the day before an examination. Everyone is in a state of (3)_________ as to what questions would be asked in the paper and whether they could write their answers properly to get good marks. Such overthinking is the cause of (4)________ which definitely affects their performance. They should revise the subject regularly and should not keep their doubts pending. This will not create a (5)________ of backlog and they will get sufficient time to resolve their problem. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Explanation: In the context of the passage, "burden" refers to a heavy load or responsibility. The idea is that if students do not revise regularly and accumulate doubts, it will create a backlog, which becomes a burden that they must deal with later. This word fits well with the meaning of the sentence. Correct sentence: "This will not create a burden of backlog and they will get sufficient time to resolve their problem."
